interactions.summary: Jiang moves from Egypt to Abu Ghraib and enhanced interrogation, rejecting the rogue-soldier explanation and saying the program was designed by psychologists working for the CIA.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang classroom lecture about modern torture; claims are his presentation of the source material.

interactions.summary: He maps learned helplessness onto the Egyptian identities: the intended experiment is to turn Ra into Osiris, but he argues torture instead radicalizes prisoners and produces ISIS-like fighters.
Jiang compares Abu Zubaydah's reported torture to the Egyptian ritual script and says the real goal was not information but programming prisoners into weapons.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang classroom lecture; ASR is noisy around names and transitions.

interactions.summary: He extends the modern continuity claim to MK Ultra, describing drugs, hypnosis, interrogation, foreign sites, destroyed records, and social control as the underlying goal.

claims.claim: Jiang says the Abu Ghraib torture was not random rogue behavior but a program designed by psychologists paid by the CIA.
Enhanced interrogation is interpreted as an experiment in learned helplessness intended to transform a Ra-like fighter into an Osiris-like passive victim.
Jiang links learned helplessness and positive psychology through Martin Seligman and provocatively calls school-taught positive psychology brainwashing.
The lecture argues that torture radicalized prisoners rather than pacifying them, connecting US-controlled prisons and torture to ISIS recruitment and extremism.
Jiang claims Abu Zubaydah's reported torture repeats the Egyptian script of force-feeding, strangling, coffin confinement, drowning, cutting, and sexual humiliation.
He says the goal of torture is not to extract information but to turn people into programmable secret weapons.
MK Ultra is presented as a 1950s and 1960s CIA program to test drugs, hypnosis, interrogation, and brainwashing on innocent citizens and through foreign intelligence settings.

signature_moments.moment: Learned helplessness becomes an attempt to turn Ra into Osiris: the fighter into the tortured victim.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It fuses the Egyptian myth frame with War on Terror psychology.

signature_moments.moment: The intended conversion reverses: instead of Ra becoming Osiris, Osiris becomes Ra.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is Jiang's account of why torture breeds the thing it claims to suppress.

signature_moments.moment: The interrogation room is recast as a drone factory for human weapons.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the lecture's hardest modern analogy.

signature_moments.moment: What was done to the pharaoh becomes a general technology: drugs, torture, ritual abuse, hypnosis, and mind control.
signature_moments.source_phrase: now they figure out how to do this to anyone
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns the ancient reconstruction into a modern mass-control claim.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A psychological theory Jiang presents as the model behind enhanced interrogation: breaking a fighter into passive victimhood.
The official label Jiang uses for torture programs he interprets as psychological experiments.
A CIA-era brainwashing and drug-testing program used by Jiang as the modern continuation of ritual programming.

chronology_notes.note: This packet is the explicit modern-history turn: 9/11, Abu Ghraib, CIA interrogation, ISIS, and MK Ultra are brought into the Egyptian framework.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR is damaged around Abu Zubaydah's name and the transition phrase 'IKEA'; do not quote those words as reliable.
ASR renders 'MK Ultra' as 'NBA ultra' in one place; context establishes the intended term.
